body:value: Till 31st August 2013, 78 entities have been appointed as Aggregator which include 26 Public Sector Banks (PSBs), 13 Regional Rural Banks (RRBs), 33 Private Entities, 2 Private Banks and 4 Labour Boards.; Under "NPS Lite Swavalamban": Aggregator is an entity appointed for enrolment of subscribers, submitting their forms and depositing their money under Swavalamban Scheme; Swavalamban Scheme is a co-contributory Pension Scheme for unorganised sector where government will contribute Rs. 1,000 to all such subscribers who are able to save Rs. 1,000 to Rs. 12,000 in a financial year. Benefits under this scheme are available in 2016-17; -Under "UOS-Swavalamban": Points of Presence (PoP) is an entity between Subscriber and Scheme; NA: Not Available.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_ministry_department:name: Ministry of Finance
Department of Financial Services
field_sector:name: Finance
Pension Reforms
title: Coverage under Swavalamban Scheme

body:value: Defence Science Journal (DSJ) is a peer-reviewed, multidisciplinary research journal in the area of defence science and technology. Journal feature recent progresses made in the field of defence/military support system and new findings/breakthroughs, etc. Major subject fields covered include: aeronautics, armaments, combat vehicles and engineering, biomedical sciences, computer sciences, electronics, material sciences, missiles, naval systems, etc.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: science and technology
field_ministry_department:name: Ministry of Defence
Department of Defence Research and Development
Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO)
field_sector:name: Defence
title: Defence Science Journal (DSJ)

body:value: The data refers to the List of High Priority Districts identified by the Department of Health and Family Welfare for Implementation of focused health care interventions under National Rural Health Mission. These districts were identified based on DLHS indicators and also included LWE districts- district with high percentage of SC/ST population etc. Bottom 25% districts within a State taken according to raking based on Composite Index plus LWE or Tribal districts falling in bottom 50%.

body:value: The data refers to state and district wise comparison of Key Indicators of District Level Household and Facility Survey. Key Indicators are Health programmes at village level- Accessibility of the health facility- Infrastructure- staff and services at Sub Centre - Infrastructure- staff and services at Primary Health Centres (PHCs) and Infrastructure- staff and services at Community Health Centres(CHCs).
